title: Multi-probe reflection + GroundedSkybox shipping bugfixes

stories.title: **REFLECTION-cube-cam-world-matrix** ‚Äî `ReflectionProbeSystem` no longer adds its `CubeCamera`s to the scene graph (three.js's‚Ä¶
stories.summary: **REFLECTION-cube-cam-world-matrix** ‚Äî `ReflectionProbeSystem` no longer adds its `CubeCamera`s to the scene graph (three.js's `CubeCamera.update()` only auto-refreshes the world matrix when `parent === null`). Explicit `cubeCam.updateMatrixWorld(true)` added in `updateReflectionProbe` so any future re-parent doesn't silently break the capture.
verification: legacy import ‚Äî see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **GROUNDED-skybox-shipping-fixes** ‚Äî `GroundedSkybox` now built with a positive projection factor `radius / 6` (matches three.js docs‚Ä¶
stories.summary: **GROUNDED-skybox-shipping-fixes** ‚Äî `GroundedSkybox` now built with a positive projection factor `radius / 6` (matches three.js docs example ‚âà 6.6); the mesh is externally positioned at `projectionHeight + spec.height` so AGF's `height: -0.75` still works as "world Y of the floor". Raw RGBE equirect feeds both `scene.background` and the helper (was the PMREM cubemap ‚Äî soft / blurred projection). Shadow-catcher lifted 10 mm above the sky's bottom disc + `renderOrder = 1` + `opacity 0.6` for natural shadow weight.
verification: legacy import ‚Äî see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **REFLECTION-cube-mipmaps** ‚Äî `WebGLCubeRenderTarget` constructed with `generateMipmaps: true, minFilter: LinearMipmapLinearFilter` so‚Ä¶
stories.summary: **REFLECTION-cube-mipmaps** ‚Äî `WebGLCubeRenderTarget` constructed with `generateMipmaps: true, minFilter: LinearMipmapLinearFilter` so `MeshStandardMaterial.envMap` sampling at `roughness > 0` reads a box-filtered mip chain. Mip regen runs once per `CubeCamera.update`. Not full PMREM GGX prefilter (parked as `REFLECTION-prefilter`), but visually close enough for moderate roughness (‚â§ 0.3).
verification: legacy import ‚Äî see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **MATERIAL-bench-multi-probe** ‚Äî three reflection probes: `sphere.centre` (chrome ball's own probe, 128¬≤ @ 30 Hz), `probe.front` at (0, 1,‚Ä¶
stories.summary: **MATERIAL-bench-multi-probe** ‚Äî three reflection probes: `sphere.centre` (chrome ball's own probe, 128¬≤ @ 30 Hz), `probe.front` at (0, 1, +5), `probe.back` at (0, 1, ‚àí5). Outer-ring spheres bind by initial angle hemisphere: `sin(angle) ‚â• 0` ‚Üí `probe.front`, else `probe.back`. Static bindings ‚Äî each sphere keeps its probe ref as the ring spins so the reflection difference between sphere groups stays visible regardless of where they orbit.
verification: legacy import ‚Äî see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **MATERIAL-bench-stonehenge** ‚Äî 12 stone-textured cylindrical columns at radius 11 around the orbit.
stories.summary: New `stone.material.json` reuses `brick_bump` (`bumpMap`, scale 2.5) + `brick_roughness` (`roughnessMap`) over `#7e7468`. Shadow camera frustum widened to ¬±14 / far 40 so columns cast onto the grounded floor.
verification: legacy import ‚Äî see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **REFLECTION-cube-cam-shadow-opacity-tune** ‚Äî live tuning loop with the user: shadow-catcher opacity 1.0 ‚Üí 0.6 (more natural shadow weight),‚Ä¶
stories.summary: **REFLECTION-cube-cam-shadow-opacity-tune** ‚Äî live tuning loop with the user: shadow-catcher opacity 1.0 ‚Üí 0.6 (more natural shadow weight), chrome roughness sweep 0.18 ‚Üí 0.02 (proved probe correctness, since mirror reflection of an offset cubemap is obviously wrong) ‚Üí 0.12 ‚Üí 0.22 (proved mip-cube blur works).
verification: legacy import ‚Äî see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **ADR-0013-reflection-probe-system** ‚Äî `docs/adr/0013-reflection-probe-system.md` anchors the CubeCamera-per-entity design, the parent-null‚Ä¶
stories.summary: **ADR-0013-reflection-probe-system** ‚Äî `docs/adr/0013-reflection-probe-system.md` anchors the CubeCamera-per-entity design, the parent-null world-matrix gotcha, the mipmap-cube-RT vs PMREM tradeoff, multi-probe layout decision for material-bench, and self-reflection avoidance.
verification: legacy import ‚Äî see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **DOCS-vfx-skill-update** ‚Äî `docs/agent/skills/vfx-authoring.md` pitfalls section picks up four new entries from the shipping bugs:‚Ä¶
stories.summary: **DOCS-vfx-skill-update** ‚Äî `docs/agent/skills/vfx-authoring.md` pitfalls section picks up four new entries from the shipping bugs: `groundedSkybox.height <= 0`, PMREM-vs-equirect for the helper, CubeCamera world-matrix gotcha, and `roughness > 0` needing the mip-cube chain.
verification: legacy import ‚Äî see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

stories.title: **GPU-timer-webgl-errors** ‚Äî `ext.QUERY_RESULT_AVAILABLE` and `ext.QUERY_RESULT` are `undefined` (the extension exposes only‚Ä¶
stories.summary: **GPU-timer-webgl-errors** ‚Äî `ext.QUERY_RESULT_AVAILABLE` and `ext.QUERY_RESULT` are `undefined` (the extension exposes only `TIME_ELAPSED_EXT`, `TIMESTAMP_EXT`, `GPU_DISJOINT_EXT`; the rest are core WebGL2 constants on the rendering context). Reading them off `ext` produced `INVALID_ENUM: getQueryParameter`. The companion `INVALID_OPERATION: endQuery: target query is not active` came from `gpuTimerPending` staying set when `beginGpuTimer` early-returned on an in-flight query. Tracked `gpuTimerActive` separately so `endQuery` only fires when `beginQuery` actually fired this frame.
verification: legacy import ‚Äî see sprint notes[] for the original sprint-level verification

followUps: **REFLECTION-prefilter (still parked)** ‚Äî three.js auto-mipmap on the cube RT is fine through `roughness ‚â§ 0.3`. Once a project needs a `roughness > 0.3` reflective material that reads correct PBR-blurry environment, vendor `PMREMGenerator.fromCubemap` and run it on each probe update. **Picked up as S59 Story 1.**

**REFLECTION-cube cam re-parenting** ‚Äî runtime path keeps the cube cam outside the scene graph; if a future feature wants to parent the probe to a moving entity, the per-frame `setReflectionProbeTransform(LocalToWorld)` already covers that; re-parenting is not actually needed.
**DOCTOR-reflection scans bootstrap entities** (carried from S57) ‚Äî material-bench's runtime-created probes still don't show up in `engine doctor`. **Picked up as S59 Story 6.**

**GPU-timer test coverage** ‚Äî the WebGL timer path has no unit-test coverage. A headless test against a mock WebGL2 ctx would have caught the `ext.QUERY_RESULT_*` bug before it reached the console. **Picked up as S59 Story 5.**
notes: Deliverables: `engine/render/three-render-adapter.ts` ‚Äî cube cam parent-null + explicit world matrix; GroundedSkybox projection factor + position split; raw equirect passed to helper; shadow-catcher z-fight fix; WebGLCubeRenderTarget mipmap config; GPU timer query parameter fix + `gpuTimerActive` gate.; `examples/material-bench/bootstrap.ts` ‚Äî 3-probe layout, outer-sphere hemisphere binding, 12 stonehenge columns.; `examples/material-bench/scenes/start.scene.json` ‚Äî dark plinth removed, `groundedSkybox: { height: -0.75, radius: 60 }`, shadow camera widened.; `examples/material-bench/assets/runtime/materials/m0-chrome.material.json` ‚Äî roughness 0.22.; `examples/material-bench/assets/runtime/materials/stone.material.json` (new).; `docs/adr/0013-reflection-probe-system.md` (new).; `docs/agent/skills/vfx-authoring.md` ‚Äî pitfalls section updated.

Verification: `npm run preflight` at sprint close ‚Äî repo:hygiene + 5 engine:check projects + imports:check + systems:check + typecheck + 83 unit test files / 505 tests + build + bundle:check + 11/11 e2e smoke (37.0 s).; User live-verified across the tuning loop: centre chrome reflects columns + orbit ring; front/back probes give visible reflection diff between orbit halves even as the ring spins; shadows land on the grounded floor; chrome roughness 0.22 reads as a softly blurred mirror; no more `INVALID_ENUM` / `INVALID_OPERATION` from the GPU timer in DevTools console.
